memory.summary: The group invitation creates friction with my academic priority, yet I will attend to avoid conflict despite the stress it adds.

desires: Complete the assigned reading by 20:00 without any social interruptions to secure a flawless academic outcome
Maintain the appearance of cooperation with cohort_3 to avoid interpersonal friction, even while feeling internally drained
Minimize cognitive load from social interactions to preserve remaining focus for the upcoming week's long-term planning
Prevent sleep debt accumulation tomorrow by strictly adhering to my sleep discipline routine despite current stress

goals.description: Politely decline cohort_3's lunch invitation via text to avoid immediate social friction while preserving energy for the assignment analysis.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' conflict style requires me to sidestep direct confrontation, but my 'achievement' value and current 'social_value' cost make attending this lunch a net negative. I will use a non-confrontational excuse (e.g., 'already ate' or 'have a deadline') to maintain the appearance of cooperation without incurring the cognitive load that threatens my focus capacity.

goals.description: Execute the assignment analysis section immediately (13:05-14:30) to clear the first hard milestone before the reading block.
goals.rationale: The plan note prioritizes finishing the analysis before reading. With a deadline of 18:00 and a 'deliberate' time horizon, completing the harder analytical task first ensures a flawless academic outcome and reduces future stress accumulation.

goals.description: Begin the assigned reading session with strict no-interruption protocols until 20:00.
goals.rationale: This is contingent on completing g2 first. The goal aligns with my primary value of 'academic' outcomes and the desire to secure a flawless result. It is deferred now because the analysis task must be cleared first to satisfy my long-term planning horizon.

goals.description: Maintain low-stress communication with cohort_3 later today to reinforce trust without engaging in deep conversation.
goals.rationale: While relationships are a lower priority than academics (0.15 vs 0.5), maintaining the baseline trust (7/7) is necessary to prevent future interpersonal friction. This is a maintenance task to be done only after high-priority academic goals are secured.
